design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect power-up capsules to advance through a power meter menu, selecting desired weapon/ability upgrades, then deploy them to overcome increasingly challenging enemy waves and boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward of power-up selection: players must decide when to activate desired upgrades vs. maintaining forward momentum, balanced against constant enemy pressure and the threat of losing accumulated power
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot the Vic Viper trans-dimensional spacecraft through alien-infested environments, progressively transforming from a weak starter ship into a heavily armed combat vessel capable of 'destroying the core'
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Revolutionary power-up system with a unified menu mechanic rather than distinct pickup items; deliberate design philosophy emphasizing visually distinct worlds, unique enemies and locations as differentiator from competitors; boss design centered around core-destruction mechanic with mandatory puzzle-like wall-breaking sequences

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with loss on collision; power-ups can be lost on death, creating significant progression setback
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system available; arcade version requires continue code input
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-stage checkpoints; stage-based progression with full restart on failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players control power-up selection timing and priority, determining ship capabilities and playstyle; can choose to advance menu selectively or hold back for specific upgrades

progression.upgrade_system: Power meter menu system: collect capsules to advance a scrolling menu of upgrades (weapons, options, shields, speed). Player selects desired upgrade by activating it at correct menu position, then menu resets. Multiple upgrade tiers available.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-by-stage linear progression; each stage completes upon core destruction. No branching paths documented.
replayability_hooks: Multiple power-up loadout strategies to optimize against different stage enemy patterns
High-score chasing on fixed level progression
Japanese version includes 6 different ending messages based on completion count (hidden acrostic spelling 'Konami')
Konami Code cheat enables full power-up access, providing meta-replayability

world_building_techniques: Visually distinct stage environments (featuring alien Moai enemies, suggesting thematic locales)
Enemy and boss design tied to environmental themes rather than cutscenes

boss_design.philosophy: Core destruction mechanic established as series-defining standard: giant organic-seeming craft with blue sphere cores at center, accessible through narrow shielded passage. Boss design operates as puzzle-like encounter requiring players to whittle destructible wall barriers through sustained, well-placed fire before accessing core. Cores change from blue to red when critically damaged. Some bosses can regenerate walls. Organic bosses feature weak points (mouth, head, eye) instead of cores.
notable_bosses: Giant Moai craft (stage 3, iconic to series lore)
Multiple core multi-phase bosses with wall-regeneration mechanics

faction_indicators: Vic Viper pilot identity (implied player role as defender)
danger_signals: Alien enemy waves with escalating complexity
Environmental hazards implied by stage-based progression
environmental_storytelling: Stage environments suggest conquered/contested alien worlds
Moai imagery in stage 3 implies cultural/archaeological themes
Boss craft design suggests weaponized megastructures
